ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists specifically for cheese castella cake; mapped to the broad 'cake food product' class as the closest defensible ancestor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Sponge cake) used as the closest defensible parent; no dedicated code exists for cheese castella cake. The cream cheese variant is not separately enumerated in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for cheese castella cake; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of cheese castella cake is one slice of approximately 80 g, as commonly portioned in Japanese bakeries and cafes.

nutrient_source.title: Cheese Castella Cake — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Cheese Castella Cake (cr_id F02010005). All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 18139 ('Cake, sponge, commercially prepared') adjusted for cream cheese content, as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; see notes.

gi_source.title: Sponge cake, plain — GI value 67 (analogous estimate for cheese castella cake)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for cheese castella cake in the University of Sydney GI Database. The GI value of 67 (medium) is adopted from the closest analogous food — plain sponge cake — as listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (67) × carbohydrate per serving (24.62 g/100g × 80 g serving = 19.7 g) / 100 = 13.2. The cream cheese component may modestly lower the effective GI relative to plain sponge cake; this is documented as a limitation.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (14.2 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.0 g/100g) drives insulin-mediated sodium retention; sodium at 320 mg/100g is borderline elevated for a dessert item.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit cheese castella cake due to its combination of high sugar (14.2 g/100g), low fibre (1.0 g/100g), and moderate sodium (320 mg/100g), all of which independently elevate blood pressure risk; if consumed, restrict to a single small slice (≤40 g) and avoid pairing with other high-sodium or high-sugar foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 14.2 g/100g approaches the 15 g/100g hard gate; dietary fibre at only 1.0 g/100g provides negligible glucose-absorption modulation; medium GI of 67 with a glycemic load of 13.2 per 80 g serving poses a meaningful postprandial glucose challenge.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid cheese castella cake or limit intake to a very small portion (30–40 g maximum) paired with a protein-rich food to blunt the postprandial glucose spike, given its high sugar content, low fibre, and medium-GI refined wheat flour base.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat of approximately 8.5 g/100g from cream cheese and butter substantially exceeds the 5 g/100g cardiovascular threshold; combined with high sugar (14.2 g/100g) this food profile is associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and triglyceride levels.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat cheese castella cake as an occasional indulgence only, limiting portions to no more than 30–40 g, as its high saturated fat (~8.5 g/100g) and high sugar (14.2 g/100g) content are independently associated with adverse lipid profiles and increased cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat (~8.5 g/100g) exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold; high sugar (14.2 g/100g) independently promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should minimise consumption of cheese castella cake as its high saturated fat (~8.5 g/100g from cream cheese and butter) and high sugar (14.2 g/100g) promote TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory pathways; if consumed at all, limit to 20–30 g on rare occasions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheese castella cake is a Japanese-origin baked dessert that adapts the traditional Portuguese-derived castella (kasutera) sponge cake by incorporating cream cheese into the batter, yielding a denser, richer, and slightly tangy product compared to the classic plain castella. The cake is made from wheat flour, eggs, sugar, cream cheese, butter, and milk, and is baked at moderate temperature to produce a golden crust with a moist, fine-crumbed interior. Per 100 g it provides 273 kcal, 10.3 g protein, 14.77 g fat (of which approximately 8.5 g is saturated fat from cream cheese and butter), 24.62 g carbohydrate, 14.2 g sugar, 1.0 g dietary fibre, 320 mg sodium, 113 mg calcium, and 1.17 mg iron. The medium glycemic index of approximately 67 and low dietary fibre content mean that blood glucose rises relatively quickly after consumption. Clinically, the food is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its high sugar, high saturated fat, and moderate sodium profile. It is commonly consumed as a snack or dessert in Japanese bakeries and has become popular across East and Southeast Asia, including Singapore.
common_user_queries: How many calories in cheese castella cake?
Can diabetics eat cheese castella cake?
What is the GI of cheese castella cake?
Is cheese castella cake suitable for hypertension?
Is cheese castella cake high in sugar?

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from the HPB Foodo database entry for Cheese Castella Cake (cr_id F02010005) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy analogous sponge cake entry and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous plain sponge cake entry and documented. Japanese local name verified from standard culinary reference.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (8.5 g/100g) is an estimate derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy item 18139 ('Cake, sponge, commercially prepared') adjusted for cream cheese content, as the HPB source does not report saturated fat breakdown. This value should be replaced with a direct measurement when available. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1 because detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this specific cheese castella cake formulation are not reported in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI value of 67 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (plain sponge cake); no direct GI measurement for cheese castella cake was found. The cream cheese component may modestly lower the effective GI. 4. Halal status is not confirmed; cream cheese and butter sourcing must be verified for halal certification. 5. FoodEx2 match type corrected from 'analogous' to 'closest_parent' (the only permitted value for a non-exact parent-level match) to satisfy G5 ontology honesty gate.

Iteration 3: Confirmed ontology.foodex2_match_type is set to 'closest_parent', which is a valid allowed value. Updated foodex2_gap_note to clarify that 'closest_parent' is used because A00LC (Sponge cake) is the nearest parent category in FoodEx2 for cheese castella cake. G5 hard gate now passes.
